text: India won the 2011 ICC Cricket World Cup, defeating Sri Lanka in the final held at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai. MS Dhoni was the captain and hit the winning six.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most runs in both Test and One Day International (ODI) cricket, and is the only player to score 100 international centuries.
The Indian Premier League (IPL) is a franchise Twenty20 cricket league founded in 2008. Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings are among the most successful franchises.
Australia has won the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup a record six times: 1987, 1999, 2003, 2007, 2015, and 2023.
A standard Test cricket match is played over five days, with each team batting twice, while an ODI is limited to 50 overs per side.
Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw. Lionel Messi was named the tournament's best player.
Lionel Messi has won the Ballon d'Or a record eight times, more than any other player in history.
Real Madrid holds the record for the most UEFA Champions League titles, having won the competition 15 times as of 2024.
The FIFA World Cup is held every four years, with Brazil being the most successful national team, having won the tournament five times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).
A standard football (soccer) match consists of two 45-minute halves, with a team fielding 11 players including a goalkeeper.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles, having surpassed Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most by any player in the Open Era.
Wimbledon, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
In tennis scoring, a game is won by reaching four points (15, 30, 40, game) with a two-point lead; a set is typically won by reaching six games.
India won its first-ever Thomas Cup title in 2022, defeating Indonesia 3-0 in the final held in Bangkok, Thailand.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics, and later won bronze at Tokyo 2020.
Lin Dan of China is one of the most successful badminton players in history, winning two Olympic gold medals and five World Championship titles.
The Badminton World Federation (BWF) organizes major events including the Thomas Cup (men's teams), Uber Cup (women's teams), and the annual World Championships.
A badminton match is typically played as the best of three games, with each game won by the first side to reach 21 points with at least a two-point lead.
The Boston Celtics hold the record for the most NBA championships, having won the title 18 times as of 2024.
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar was the NBA's all-time leading scorer until LeBron James surpassed his record in February 2023.
A standard NBA basketball game consists of four 12-minute quarters, while FIBA international games use four 10-minute quarters.
The 1992 US Olympic men's basketball team, known as the 'Dream Team,' featured Michael Jordan, Magic Johnson, and Larry Bird and won gold at the Barcelona Olympics.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls and was named Finals MVP in all six of those title runs.
